title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.articleTitle: The Role of Curiosity in Scientific Discovery
content.text: Curiosity is often (0) described as the engine that drives scientific progress. It is the desire to understand the unknown that (1) researchers to spend years investigating obscure phenomena. While many discoveries are the result of (2) planning, some of the most significant breakthroughs have occurred by accident. This phenomenon, known as serendipity, happens when a scientist (3) across an unexpected result while looking for something else. However, for serendipity to (4) a discovery, the researcher must possess an observant and open mind. As Louis Pasteur famously (5) , "chance favors only the prepared mind." This means that curiosity must be (6) with a rigorous methodology to produce meaningful results. Without a systematic approach, an unexpected finding might simply be (7) as a mistake. Today, fostering a culture of curiosity is more important than ever as we face complex global challenges. By encouraging students to ask "why" and "how," we can (8) a new generation of thinkers capable of solving the mysteries of the future. After all, the history of science proves that a simple question can often lead to a revolution in our understanding of the universe and its laws.

example.explanation: “Described as” is the standard way to define or characterize a concept.

questions.explanation: “Compels” is a formal verb for a strong inner drive or feeling that makes you do something.
“Meticulous planning” is a very common academic collocation meaning very careful and detailed.
“Stumbles across” is a phrasal verb meaning to find something by chance.
“Lead to” means to result in a particular outcome; it fits the syntax of the sentence.
“Remarked” is a formal way to introduce a quote or a specific statement.
“Coupled with” is a formal expression meaning combined with something else.
“Dismissed as” means to decide that something is not important or not worth considering.
“Nurture a generation” means to encourage the development and growth of a group.
